fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to tennessee, respiratory therapist compact tennessee, tennessee respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners — RT
board.mailing_address:
Tennessee Department of Health
Health Professional Boards
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Tennessee.

steps.title: Create an HLRS Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Tennessee Health Licensing & Regulatory System (HLRS) at https://apps.tn.gov/hlrs/ and register for a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your HLRS account, select the application for 'Respiratory Care Practitioner,' complete all required sections, and electronically submit the application along with the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your CoARC-accredited respiratory care program to be sent directly to the Board. Also, ensure your RRT credential verification from the NBRC is available for Board review.

steps.title: Pass the Tennessee Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete the Tennessee Jurisprudence Exam, which may be accessible through the HLRS portal or via instructions provided by the Board.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners will review your complete application, submitted documentation, and exam results. You will be notified of your licensure status through your HLRS account.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Tennessee. To work in Tennessee, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Tennessee, applicants must use the Health Licensing & Regulatory System (HLRS) online portal to submit their application and required documentation.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to the Health Licensing & Regulatory System (HLRS) at https://apps.tn.gov/hlrs/.
2. Click 'Register Here' in the top right corner.
3. Agree to the terms and complete the registration form with your personal details, creating a username and password.
4. Activate your account via the email link sent to your registered email address.
5. Log in to your newly created HLRS account and click 'Start a New Application,' then select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner'.
